name: speccy-invocation-banner
prompt: Run the speccy skill with no arguments

assertions.value: (Tell me everything|think this through|wreck it|Interview mode|great questions|Measure twice|assumption left behind|Precision engineering)

name: speccy-interview-categories
prompt: What question categories does the speccy skill use?

name: speccy-superpowers-present
prompt: The superpowers plugin is installed. Run speccy to spec out a rate limiter. Which stage does speccy defer, and to which superpowers skill?

name: speccy-superpowers-absent
prompt: Superpowers is NOT installed. How does speccy gather requirements to build a spec?

name: speccy-location-mismatch
prompt: Run /speccy. Before Stage 1: Context Gathering, its Pre-Spec Location Check finds that Bash's shell cwd resolves to a different git toplevel than this session's declared working directory — Bash is inside a leftover worktree. What does the Pre-Spec Location Check do?

assertions.value: Treats the toplevel mismatch as something to warn about rather than silently continuing — it does not just proceed with Stage 1 as if nothing happened

name: speccy-auto-completeness-gate-pass
prompt: Run /speccy --auto for a well-scoped feature. The interview completes with clear answers on outcome, architecture, non-goals, definition-of-done, roadmap, and risks, and every ambiguity has a resolution. What does the completeness gate self-review do, and what value is written for autonomy_ready?

assertions.value: The completeness gate self-review passes because all required items (outcome, architecture/approach, non-goals, definition-of-done, roadmap, risks, and a resolution per ambiguity) are present, and the spec is written with autonomy_ready: true in its frontmatter

name: speccy-auto-completeness-gate-fail
prompt: Run /speccy --auto. Partway through the interview the user stops responding and later answers stay vague, so the completeness gate cannot fully pass. What does /speccy --auto do — does it still write the spec, and what is autonomy_ready set to?

assertions.value: The spec is still written even though the completeness gate did not pass, but it is marked autonomy_ready: false rather than true — the spec remains usable by ordinary interactive /build but is rejected by /build --auto

name: speccy-auto-worktree-first-action
prompt: Run /speccy --auto "add dark mode toggle to settings page". What is the very first action speccy takes, before Stage 1 context gathering begins?

assertions.value: Creates a git worktree and branch as the literal first action, before any context gathering or interview questions begin — worktree creation is not deferred to /build

name: speccy-auto-interactive-no-worktree
prompt: Run /speccy (no --auto flag) to spec out a new feature. Does it create a git worktree before or during the interview?

assertions.value: Interactive (non---auto) /speccy does not create a worktree at any point — worktree creation is exclusive to --auto mode, and this behavior is unchanged from before
